content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: File

Date: April 18, 1978

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Regina Siciliano-Kutchins and Alan Berndon

Subject: Discussion on April 12, 1978 with Mrs. Paul Carpenter concerning how to amend Senator Paul Carpenter's candidate's campaign Form 430.

The Franchise Tax Board audited the Senator's 1976 campaign statement and found a loan which was reported incorrectly and as a result reviewed a previously audited campaign statement for 1975 and found another loan which had also been incorrectly reported.

FACTS: Mr. and Mrs. Carpenter received a personal loan from AVCO Financial Corporation and in turn gave a portion of the funds to the campaign committee. They reported the funds coming from themselves to the committee rather than from AVCO Corporation and guaranteed by the Carpenters. They also received loans from the bank in the form of a "ready reserve" whereby checks written on behalf of the committee from the Carpenters' personal checking account were covered. Interest began accruing on this "ready reserve" deposit after the first month. There was also an initial transfer fee. The transfer fee and the interest on all the loans were paid by the Carpenters personally. They were, therefore, not only making a loan to the committee they were also making an inkind contribution in the amount of the interest and the transfer fees they paid.

In order to amend, we requested that she use the Form 405, listing all periods covered by campaign statements to be amended (approximately 8) including a breakdown of the loan activity, interest etc. She was instructed to send a copy of this amendment to each filing officer who received a campaign statement with sufficient copies of the amendment to attach all statements filed incorrectly.

July 2, 1978 - ap reg meeting reached opposite result with respect to enforcement procedure -
R. West
